findings.title: Unsafe `as McpServerConfig` cast in update() breaks union safety
findings.description: Encapsulation: 7/10, Expression: 6/10, Usefulness: 6/10, Enforcement: 4/10 (Aggregate: 5.75). The spread of `Partial<McpServerConfigInput>` onto an existing `McpServerConfig` produces an untyped object that is force-cast with `as McpServerConfig`. Because McpServerConfig is a discriminated union, the compiler cannot verify the merged result is a valid variant. For example, spreading `{ transport: 'sse', url: '...' }` onto a `StdioServerConfig` would produce an object with both `command` and `url` fields -- an illegal state the union is specifically designed to prevent. The subsequent `validateConfig(merged)` catches some issues at runtime, but the cast bypasses the type system's compile-time protection entirely.
findings.suggestion: Instead of spreading and casting, reconstruct the config through `buildConfig()` using the merged input values. Parse the merged partial back into a `McpServerConfigInput` first, then call `buildConfig(base, mergedInput)` to get a properly typed `McpServerConfig`. This ensures the discriminated union is constructed through a validated code path.

findings.title: McpTransport union mixes class instance with plain objects
findings.description: Encapsulation: 5/10, Expression: 4/10, Usefulness: 6/10, Enforcement: 5/10 (Aggregate: 5.0). The McpTransport union mixes a class instance (Experimental_StdioMCPTransport) that lacks a `type` discriminant with plain objects that have `type: 'sse' | 'http'`. This makes type narrowing impossible via a single discriminant field. Consumers must use `instanceof` for the stdio variant and property checks for others. This forces the `transport as McpTransport` cast at line 21 of mcp-client-manager.ts because the SDK's createMCPClient cannot accept the heterogeneous union directly.
findings.suggestion: Wrap the stdio transport in an object with a `type: 'stdio'` discriminant, e.g., `{ type: 'stdio'; transport: Experimental_StdioMCPTransport }`. This makes the union consistently discriminated on `type` and eliminates the need for `as` casts when passing to the SDK. Alternatively, since `createTransport` is only consumed by `McpClientManager.connect()`, make it return the SDK-expected type directly and remove the exported `McpTransport` union.

findings.title: getTools/aggregateTools return Record<string, unknown> losing type info
findings.description: Encapsulation: 8/10, Expression: 3/10, Usefulness: 4/10, Enforcement: 5/10 (Aggregate: 5.0). Both `getTools()` and `aggregateTools()` return `Record<string, unknown>`, discarding the SDK's tool definition type information. The `@ai-sdk/mcp` client's `.tools()` method returns typed tool definitions that include parameter schemas, descriptions, and execute functions. Collapsing this to `unknown` forces every consumer to perform unsafe casts or runtime checks to use the tools, defeating the purpose of the typed SDK.
findings.suggestion: Use the SDK's return type: `type McpTools = Awaited<ReturnType<McpClient['tools']>>` and use `McpTools` as the return type for both methods. This preserves the full tool definition types through the hub facade and allows consumers to use tools without casting.

findings.title: No Valibot schema validation at Imperative Shell boundary
findings.description: Encapsulation: 7/10, Expression: 6/10, Usefulness: 7/10, Enforcement: 5/10 (Aggregate: 6.25). The project standard is Valibot for validation (per CLAUDE.md), but McpServerStore uses a hand-rolled `validateConfig()` function. While the manual validation covers basic cases (empty name, missing command/url, shell metacharacter injection), it lacks: URL format validation for SSE/HTTP transports, header key/value sanitization, and name length limits. A Valibot schema would provide declarative, composable validation that is easier to maintain and test, and would produce consistent error formats across the codebase.
findings.suggestion: Define Valibot schemas for McpServerConfigInput variants (e.g., `StdioServerConfigInputSchema = v.object({ transport: v.literal('stdio'), command: v.pipe(v.string(), ...), ... })`). Use `v.union([StdioSchema, SseSchema, HttpSchema])` as the discriminated validator. The command allowlist and shell metachar checks can be custom Valibot validators. This aligns with the project's validation standard and makes the constraints declarative.

findings.title: McpServerConfig interfaces lack readonly modifiers
findings.code_snippet:
interface McpServerConfigBase {
  id: string
  name: string
  enabled: boolean
  createdAt: number
  updatedAt: number
}
findings.description: Encapsulation: 6/10, Expression: 7/10, Usefulness: 7/10, Enforcement: 6/10 (Aggregate: 6.5). McpServerConfigBase and all derived interfaces (StdioServerConfig, SseServerConfig, HttpServerConfig) use mutable fields. Since these represent persisted configuration read from the database, they should be treated as immutable values -- mutations should only occur through the store's update() method. Adding `readonly` to all fields would prevent accidental mutation by consumers and reinforce the store-as-single-source-of-truth pattern.
findings.suggestion: Mark all fields in McpServerConfigBase as `readonly`. Since the derived interfaces inherit these fields and add their own, also mark the transport-specific fields as `readonly`. Alternatively, use `Readonly<McpServerConfigBase>` as the base or wrap the exported type: `export type McpServerConfig = Readonly<StdioServerConfig> | Readonly<SseServerConfig> | Readonly<HttpServerConfig>`.

positive_observations: McpServerConfig is now a proper discriminated union on `transport` field with StdioServerConfig, SseServerConfig, and HttpServerConfig variants -- a significant improvement from iter1's flat interface with optional fields.
McpServerConfigInput mirrors the discriminated union pattern, ensuring input validation aligns with the config structure.
safeRowToConfig() uses a proper isTransportType() type guard and exhaustive switch instead of unsafe `as` cast -- eliminates the previous iter1 P1 for unsafe DB row deserialization.
validateConfig() now runs on both add() and update() paths, closing the iter1 gap where update() had no validation.
Command allowlist (ALLOWED_COMMANDS) and shell metacharacter detection (SHELL_METACHAR_PATTERN) provide defense-in-depth for stdio transport security.
McpClientManager has good encapsulation with private Map and proper cleanup in disconnectAll() using Promise.allSettled.
ServerStatus is a focused 2-value literal union; ConnectEnabledResult provides structured success/failure reporting.
